title: Pteris ornithopus
taxonomy: Pteris ornithopus is classified as a species of fern in the family Pteridaceae. This plant is commonly known as the Australian bird's nest fern. It belongs to the genus Pteris, which consists of over 100 species of ferns. Pteris ornithopus is native to Australia and is often found growing in forests and woodlands. It is a climbing fern that uses its long stems to ascend trees and other supports.
uses: Pteris ornithopus, commonly known as the Australian bracken fern, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. Its leaves have been employed in the treatment of various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Pteris ornithopus for medicinal purposes. In some parts of the world, it is also used as a food source, with its fiddleheads being edible.

side_effects: Pteris ornithopus, also known as the lighthouse fern or common button fern, is generally considered a non-toxic and safe plant for indoor environments. intro: Pteris ornithopus, also known as the button fern or bird's-foot fern, is a species of fern belonging to the genus Pteris. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are said to help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, reduce stress, and protect against oxidative damage. The bioactive constituents of Pteris ornithopus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
